title: Prenatal/Postnatal Home Visitation Programs * At Risk Families - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Health Care > Prenatal/Postnatal Home Visitation Programs * At Risk Families 19 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter African American Community At Risk Families No Filter African American Community At Risk Families 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details DESCHUTES COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH BABIES FIRST! Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A public health nurse (PHN) home visiting program that partners with families to improve pregnancy, family and child health outcomes. Provides health and developmental screenings, health and nutrition education, infant and child development tips, breastfeeding support, and referrals to community resources that center on the family's needs. 2577 NE Courtney Drive Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Perinatal people, infants and children 0-5, and parent or primary caregivers with a higher risk for health issues. Risk factors may include prematurity, teenage parents, at-risk caregiver, domestic violence, drug-exposed infants. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 322-7400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY DEVELOPMENT CENTER DOUGLAS COUNTY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive family support programs, in-home support and counseling, crisis intervention, parent education classes and support groups, community resource and referrals, and play therapy for children. Offers classes for young children with trained teachers. 300 Jerry's Drive Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 673-4354 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GILLIAM COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH BABIES FIRST! Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 440-3622 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VOLUNTEERS OF AMERICA OREGON FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY EAST PORTLAND FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting education, support through home visits, and group activities and intensive case management. Children participate in therapeutic classrooms with low adult-child ratios, and families receive home visits that promote healthy parenting and child development. 2609 SE 136th Avenue Portland, OR 97236 Eligibility: High risk families with children ages 6 weeks to 5 years old. Intakes are completed to determine eligibility. Hours: Therapeutic Early Childhood Program: Monday-Thursday 9:30am-12:30pm Parent Education Classes: Offered quarterly one afternoon a week Respite Care: Monday and Wednesday afternoon for home based and waitlisted families Email (503) 972-9643 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WASHINGTON COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH BABIES FIRST!
